text: Nature Scientific Reports (April 2025) benchmarked machine learning and deep learning models for network intrusion detection — evaluating 12 algorithms (Random Forest, XGBoost, DNN, CNN-LSTM hybrid) on CSE-CIC-IDS2018 and UNSW-NB15 datasets — finding that ensemble deep learning models achieve 98.5% detection rate with 2.1% false positive rate, but performance degrades to 72% on zero-day attacks not represented in training data.

Frontiers in AI (September 2025) presented a hybrid anomaly-based NIDS integrating multiple ML and DL models with an ensemble meta-classifier — demonstrating 99.1% accuracy on known attacks and 85% detection rate on novel attack types by combining supervised anomaly detection (identifying deviations from normal traffic patterns learned via autoencoders) with signature-based detection.
